storyDialogue.text: Get started! Use ‣ AUTO for auto scene change, LEFT/RIGHT icon for manual navigation, and toggle music/voice on the top bar. Explore character profiles via the menu.
I want this clean, Runner. No traces, no witnesses, and definitely no mercy.
Coordinates locked in. You’re getting close, but something’s off… Keep your eyes sharp. The static in this sector is heavy.
Visual’s glitching… [shake] Runner, do you copy? I'm losing your bio-sign!
[From the shadows] You shouldn’t have come here, Corp dog. This alley belongs to the forgotten.
[shake] A sudden electrical surge rips through your implants. Pain white-outs your vision as you collapse to the cold pavement.
Eva, look! He’s collapsing. Did we... did we hit him too hard?
Let him rot. Corporate trash like him don’t deserve a quick end. They didn't give one to our parents.
Eva, he’s totally passed out. We should do something, right? We can't just leave a body in front of the hideout.
Fine. Tie him up. We’re not playing nice with assassins. If he wakes up, I'll deal with him personally.
The signal fades into a hum of static as the world goes black. [Signal lost]
Oh, you’re awake! I thought you'd sleep longer... after what we did. I’m Tia, by the way! Don't try to move, the zip-ties are military grade.
[shake] Tia, don’t get friendly with him! He’s dangerous. We should’ve flatlined him the moment he hit the floor.
Corporate goons like him have one purpose—getting rid of us. Stop acting like he’s your new pet.
But he doesn’t *seem* bad… I mean, look at his eyes. I want to know why he came after us. Who sent you?
This isn’t a negotiation, Tia. You know how this ends. Once we get what we need, he's obsolete.
You stare at the two sisters—one full of innocent curiosity, the other radiating lethal intent. Why did the Boss want them dead? The choice you make next determines if you're a hunter or a human.
